title: Historical Bounded Parallel Work Governance And Disjoint Scope Planning
historical_note: This packet records one bounded Olares Phase 5 parallel-work governance and disjoint-scope planning slice from before the canonical repo boundary moved to `C:/APEX Platform/apex-power-ops-platform` on 2026-05-07. It remains packet-history provenance, not a live execution instruction for current repo operations.
current_routing: use PROJECT_STATUS.md for the current residue-retirement lane and latest completed packets
use docs/architecture/OLARES-PUBLICATION-BOUNDARY-RETIREMENT-DEPENDENCY-INVENTORY-2026-05-06.md for the remaining post-cutover boundary closeout queue
use this packet JSON only when historical provenance is needed for the earlier `olares-phase-5-059` parallel-work governance and disjoint-scope planning slice
objective: Define the first safe Olares Phase 5 parallel-work pilot shape after Packet 058, including disjoint ownership, coordinator publication rules, validation gates, conflict handling, and no-go boundaries, without executing source edits or approving migration.
dependency_note: Packet 058 determined Phase 5 is ready for parallel-work planning but not actual parallel source/test execution.
required_inputs: Three prior bounded source/test publication cycles
Known no-go boundaries for migration, runtime/service mutation, package mutation, AI-services, Gitea/code-hosting, canonical-hosting, remote rewrite, rollback, and old-clone mutation
constrained_outputs: A planning handoff defining whether a first parallel pilot should have two workers or remain serial
Candidate disjoint file/task slices for a first pilot, or a decision that no candidate is safe yet
Coordinator-owned publication and host reconciliation rules

No source edits, no host mutation, no publication, no package or lockfile mutation, no install, no runtime or service mutation, no remote rewrite, no AI-services expansion, no Gitea/code-hosting transition, no canonical-hosting transition, no migration approval, no rollback, no force/reset/clean, and no old-clone mutation
validation_steps: Identify candidate disjoint work slices or state why no first parallel pilot is safe.
Define coordinator, worker, validation, merge, conflict, and publication responsibilities.
State the exact next packet: authority publication, parallel pilot execution, further planning, or defer.
sql_permission_note: This packet authorizes no database writes and no source edits, host mutation, publication, package mutation, lockfile mutation, install, runtime mutation, service mutation, remote rewrite, AI-services expansion, Gitea/code-hosting transition, canonical-hosting transition, migration approval, rollback, force/reset/clean, or old-clone mutation.
closure_note: Executed 2026-05-04. Packet 059 defined the first safe parallel-work pilot as coordinator-owned governance/publication plus at most one mutation worker at a time, because the known apparatus and relay source/test slices both converge on `apps/operations-web/tests/browser-shell.smoke.spec.ts` and therefore are not safely disjoint for simultaneous multi-worker mutation. Packet 059 preserved no-migration and no-runtime/package/remote/AI-services/Gitea/canonical-hosting/old-clone boundaries, defined validation and abort rules, and selected Packet 060 as the next authority publication and host-mirror resync gate before any later parallel execution decision opens.
next_action: Open only Packet 060 as the Packet 058 and Packet 059 authority publication and host-mirror resync gate. Do not treat Packet 059 planning as parallel source/test execution authority, migration approval, host mutation, publication, package/toolchain repair, runtime/service mutation, AI-services expansion, Gitea/code-hosting transition, canonical-hosting transition, remote rewrite, rollback, force/reset/clean, or old-clone mutation.
